audience: leaders considering a more structured hiring process
title: A clearer hiring conversation starts before the interview

claims.text: A role scorecard and consistent interview questions can make the evidence conversation easier to follow.

draft: A hiring process becomes easier to review when the team agrees on the role outcome and the evidence it will look for before interviews begin. A clear scorecard does not replace judgment; it gives the people making the decision a more consistent conversation. If your team is preparing a role, start by defining the outcome, the observable evidence, and who owns each decision.
call_to_action: Invite the reader to request an exploratory conversation about role and process clarity.
boundaries: No client story, promised result, testimonial, or market claim is included.
This draft does not publish, send, schedule, or make an employment decision.
The research source is used for internal educational context, not legal advice.
